title: Xpeng Aridge Flying Car Draws 90+ Daily Orders at Beijing Auto Show — 7,000 Total Pre-Orders; He Xiaopeng Targets FSD Lead Over Tesla by August 2026
detail: Xpeng's dedicated flying car subsidiary Aridge drew record crowds and exceeded 90 orders in a single day at Auto China 2026 on Sunday April 27, bringing total pre-orders for the 'Land Aircraft Carrier' modular flying car to 7,000 units. The Aridge product concept features a car body with a detachable aerial module capable of carrying passengers; Xpeng has targeted 2027 mass production at a price point accessible to affluent individual buyers — making it the world's first civilian flying car positioned for mass market sale rather than pure concept demonstration. Alongside the order momentum, Xpeng CEO He Xiaopeng made a significant competitive declaration: Xpeng's intelligent driving system will surpass Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) in China by August 2026. This target — public and time-bound — reflects the dramatic reversal of the competitive dynamic in autonomous vehicle development, where Chinese regulators and consumers have enabled faster real-world deployment of autonomous features than the US regulatory environment. The GX robotaxi's four in-house Turing chips delivering 3,000 TOPS uses Xpeng's Vehicle-Language-Action (VLA) 2.0 physical AI system, processing sensor data end-to-end without modular hand-offs. With 98.52% of new Xpeng owners activating intelligent driving features in their first week, Xpeng's data advantage compounds rapidly. The flying car's commercial traction at Auto China 2026 validates China's unique regulatory and consumer environment for emerging mobility forms: where Western regulators are still debating eVTOL certification frameworks, Chinese consumers are placing pre-orders for 2027 delivery. The Aridge flying car alongside IRON humanoid robot production (Q4 2026) and the GX robotaxi represent the three pillars of Xpeng's physical AI ecosystem — the most comprehensive integrated mobility-AI-robotics platform from a single Chinese company.

media.caption: Xpeng's Aridge flying car draws crowds and 90+ daily orders at Auto China 2026 — 7,000 pre-orders total as CEO targets FSD lead over Tesla by August

title: Auto China 2026 Day 4 — People's Daily: 'Intelligence Is the New Horsepower'; China Sells 12.1M EVs in 2025; AI-First Cars Redefine Global Industry Standard
detail: By Day 4 of Auto China 2026 (April 27), China's state media captured the emerging consensus from the world's largest auto show: 'Intelligence is the new horsepower'. People's Daily's headline reflected how Chinese automakers had reframed the global competition — shifting from raw battery range and price as the battleground to AI integration, physical intelligence, and full-stack domestic technology. BYD's 2025 annual milestone of 12.1 million EV and plug-in hybrid vehicles sold cemented its position as the world's undisputed EV volume leader by a wide margin. Across the show floor, every major Chinese brand was showcasing complete domestic AI chip stacks designed to eliminate Nvidia dependency in automotive applications: Xpeng's Turing chips, Horizon Robotics' Starry 6 SoC (integrating cockpit and driving functions), BYD's own automotive NPUs, and Leapmotor's AI computing platforms. Digitimes analysis noted that Chinese automakers have entered a phase of 'AI-first' design where the compute architecture and AI models are designed before the vehicle body — inverting the traditional automotive development process. OMODA & JAECOO's 1 million unit global sales milestone (announced April 26-27) underscores how rapidly second-tier Chinese brands are displacing Japanese and Korean competitors in Global South markets. Pony.ai outlined plans for lower-cost Gen-7 robotaxi vehicles targeting deployment in China and the Middle East. The show's record scale — 380,000 square meters, 1,451 vehicles, 181 world premieres from global and Chinese brands — reflects the gravitational shift: Auto China is now the world's most important auto show by innovation density, superseding Detroit and Geneva. China's auto sector has achieved something unprecedented: complete vertical integration from battery materials (lithium, cobalt processing) through cells, packs, motors, AI chips, LLM models, and full vehicle platforms — all domestically produced, increasingly competitive at the frontier, and scaling globally.

media.caption: Auto China 2026: Chinese automakers redefine global automotive competition with AI-first design, domestic chips, and integrated physical AI ecosystems
